channel.title: Amsterdam on yuejn
channel.description: Recent content in Amsterdam on yuejn

item.title: Supporting local businesses during the pandemic
item.description: I have neither the knowledge nor the bandwidth to discuss how the pandemic is impacting the local Amsterdam economy. All I know is that the places below have (re)designed themselves in line with government guidance, in order to keep providing and selling their wares. These are places that I have patroned before, have done during this crisis, or hope to do so in the near future. If there is a place that I really want to shop, eat, or drink from — I try to find them directly on the website/social media pages and even sometimes call to see if I can pick something up directly from them, or purchase a voucher (#helpdehoreca).

item.title: Month-by-month listing of events of interest
item.description: A month-by-month list of events that usually happen in Amsterdam and surrounding areas. Check the event’s website for more up-to-date information! And this is totally not a comprehensive list – a mix of theatre, art, film, liquor, food, and music. January First Art Fair L & B Whiskey Weekend National Tulip Day Hotel Nacht, an evening where a number of hotels offer reduced rates to Amsterdammers to spend the night Vondelparkloop, a race around Vondelpark Ice * Amsterdam, an outdoor ice-rink on Museumplein every third Sunday of the month, begins Museum Markt February Impro Amsterdam, an international improv festival Little Italy highlights Italian food, drink, and music Mono Japan celebrates contemporary Japanese craft and design Human Rights Weekend, participate in a weekend of films, discussions, and photography (English) Huishoudbeurs, a homemakers’ fair Chocoa Festival brings together the whole chocolate supply chain from cocoa farmers to cocoa consumers Opera Forward Festival is two weeks of new works, original initiatives, and young talents of Dutch National Opera March Jazzfest, for Amsterdam jazz musicians from different schools under one roof The Whiskey & Spirits Festival The Amsterdam Coffee Festival CinemAsia Film Festival, enhancing Asian visibility in culture and media Cinedans - Dance on Screen Festival Something Raw, international dance and performance festival Roze Filmdagen, LGBTQ film festival Cross-linx Festival, a travelling indie-classical festival where avant-garde pop and contemporary music meet Amsterdam Wine Festival Open Tower Day where the public is able to visit many of the city’s monumental towers Goois Jazz Festival Pure Markt, where artisan entrepreneurs go to talk and sell good food and drinks with a little arts, crafts, and music thrown in National Restaurant Week is a time when you can enjoy a special 3-course dinner for less than €30,00 April It’s flower season!

item.title: The best work/study-friendly coffee spots in Amsterdam
item.description: As a former student, freelancer, and perennial side-gigger, I’ve gathered a list of decent places to work/study from that is not my home. I never went fully freelance, so I stuck to spots where I could sit for free, or for the price of a coffee. Other criteria was based on stable Wi-Fi, outlet availability, and decent seating. All serve excellent coffee. The Hoxton (Herengracht 255) is one of the most famous working spots in the city.

item.title: Tips and tricks for enjoying city culture
item.description: If you’re into concerts, galleries, and museums, you’re in a good place. The first tip is – forget about expensive resellers with 40% booking fees. Ticketswap is an excellent Amsterdam-based platform that lets you buy and sell tickets at a fair price. Sellers are only allowed to ask up to 20% above the original price. I am also a fan of Tiqets but always double-check the prices – sometimes it’s more than the original venue.

item.title: Nice things to know when you've just moved
item.description: Administration Registration If you plan to stay in the Netherlands for more than four months, you need to register at the town hall of the municipality you’re staying. This is called the BSN (burgerservicenummer). It’s pretty important to have – especially if you want to get paid and open up a bank account. You can take an appointment even before finding a room, as you will most likely only get an appointment three weeks away from the day you call.

item.title: A guide to finding a room on a budget
item.description: This is a guide for people who are looking for a room in a shared house/apartment in Amsterdam. It was first written in 2016 with sporadic updates through to 2019. This advice mostly applies to the non-Dutch, i.e. internationals, who are looking for long-term housing in a shared house. There are four things to keep in mind of while searching for a place to stay: Have a budget and move-in date/month.
